revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。[[日本武尊]]の妃・[[弟橘媛]...'
revision.text:
'''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。[[日本武尊]]の妃・[[弟橘媛]]の父として『日本書紀』に記される'''穂積忍山宿禰'''と同一人物とされる。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜<ref>宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref> == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
*父：[[大水口宿禰]]（[[穂積氏]]遠祖） *母：[[葛城宇那昆姫]] **子：[[大木別垂根]] **娘：[[弟橘媛]] **娘：[[弟財郎女]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
=== 参考文献 === * 宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしやまたりね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

'''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。[[日本武尊]]の妃・[[弟橘媛]]の父として『日本書紀』に記される'''穂積忍山宿禰'''と同一人物とされる。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜<ref>宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref> == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
*父：[[大水口宿禰]]（[[穂積氏]]遠祖） *母：[[葛城宇那昆姫]] **子：[[大木別垂根]]‐穂積臣氏祖・[[穂積真津]]の父。 **娘：[[弟橘媛]] **娘：[[弟財郎女]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
=== 参考文献 === * 宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしやまたりね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

'''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。[[日本武尊]]の妃・[[弟橘媛]]の父として『日本書紀』に記される'''穂積忍山宿禰'''と同一人物とされる。垂根の[[ネ (称号)]]は原始的カバネの一つ。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜<ref>宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref> == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。

[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜<ref>宝賀寿男 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』 古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref> == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
*父：[[大水口宿禰]]（[[穂積氏]]遠祖） *母：[[葛城氏|葛城宇那昆姫]] **子：[[大木別垂根]]‐穂積臣氏祖・[[穂積真津]]の父。 **娘：[[弟橘媛]] **娘：[[弟財郎女]]
== 記録 == [[古事記]]成務条において、[[成務天皇]]の妃である[[弟財郎女]]の父として登場する。また[[日本書紀]]に記される、[[日本武尊]]の妃・[[弟橘媛]]の父である穂積忍山宿禰も同一人物とされる。

{{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしやまたりね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 建忍山垂根 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 穂積氏忍山宿禰（日本書紀） | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[成務天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 =[[大水口宿禰]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 =大木別垂根、[[弟財郎女]]<ref>『古事記』中巻 成務天皇段</ref>、[[大橘比売命]]、[[弟橘媛|弟橘比売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。娘に[[弟財郎女]]がおり、[[成務天皇]]の后となったという。
== 記録 == 『[[古事記]]』成務条において、[[成務天皇]]の妃である[[弟財郎女]]の父として登場する。また『古事記』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[常陸国風土記]]』に記される、[[倭建命]]の妃である[[大橘比売命]]・[[弟橘比売命]]姉妹の父でもある。穂積神社、忍山神社の祭神。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}}, {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}}, {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}}, {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}}, {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}}, {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命}} and {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社}}.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:建忍山垂根 up to rev 103100745
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 建忍山垂根 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 穂積氏忍山宿禰（日本書紀） | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[成務天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 =[[大水口宿禰]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 =大木別垂根、[[弟財郎女]]<ref>『古事記』中巻 成務天皇段</ref>、[[大橘比売命]]、[[弟橘媛|弟橘比売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。娘に[[弟財郎女]]がおり、[[成務天皇]]の后となったという。
== 記録 == 『[[古事記]]』成務条において、[[成務天皇]]の妃である[[弟財郎女]]の父として登場する。また『古事記』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[常陸国風土記]]』に記される、[[倭建命]]の妃である[[大橘比売命]]・[[弟橘比売命]]姉妹の父でもある。穂積神社、忍山神社の祭神。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 ==
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[建忍山垂根]] to [[Keninoyama Tarine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 建忍山垂根 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 穂積氏忍山宿禰（日本書紀） | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[成務天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 =[[大水口宿禰]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 =大木別垂根、[[弟財郎女]]<ref>『古事記』中巻 成務天皇段</ref>、[[大橘比売命]]、[[弟橘媛|弟橘比売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''建忍山垂根'''（たけおしやまたりね）は、『[[古事記]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の祖とされる。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。娘に[[弟財郎女]]がおり、[[成務天皇]]の后となったという。
== 記録 == 『[[古事記]]』成務条において、[[成務天皇]]の妃である[[弟財郎女]]の父として登場する。また『古事記』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[常陸国風土記]]』に記される、[[倭建命]]の妃である[[大橘比売命]]・[[弟橘比売命]]姉妹の父でもある。穂積神社、忍山神社の祭神。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 ==
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしやまたりね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

Iratsume|qq=|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|qq=|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|qq=|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people in the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|qq=|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|qq=|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.

He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.

Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:建忍山垂根]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:建忍山垂根]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:建忍山垂根]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ototachibanahime|WD=Q11487396}}; {{ill|Q106382454|WD=Q106382454}}|father={{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041189]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041189]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omikuchi no Sukune|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]].
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people in the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=ototakaranoiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Takeoshi Yama Tarine | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Hozumi no Oshiyama Sukune (as recorded in Nihon Shoki) | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}} ancestor | parents = {{ill|Omikuchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omikuchi no Sukune|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = Omikibetsu Tarine, {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=Oto-takara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}} | notable_activities = }}
'''Takeoshi Yama Tarine''' (建忍山垂根) is a figure mentioned in ancient Japanese texts such as the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}''. He is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == The genealogy of Takeoshi Yama Tarine is not detailed in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' or the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}''. However, he is identified as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=Oto-takara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, who became an empress of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}.
He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=Oto-takara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people in the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

He is also recognized as the father of the sisters {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, both of whom are associated with the legendary prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} as recorded in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Historical Mentions == In the ''Kojiki'' section about Emperor Seimu, Takeoshi Yama Tarine is introduced as the father of {{ill|Otodokoro no Iratsume|ja|弟財郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otodokoro no Iratsume|1=Oto-takara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20039293}}, a consort of the emperor. Additionally, he is described in the ''Kojiki'', ''Nihon Shoki'', and ''Hitachi Fudoki'' as the father of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|大橘比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛}} and {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}. These sisters are featured prominently in the mythology surrounding Yamato Takeru.
Takeoshi Yama Tarine is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Hozumi Shrine|ja|穂積神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshiyama Shrine|ja|忍山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiyama Shrine|qq=}}.
